content.claim: Hungary has developed assessment infrastructure (National ABC testing at grades 6, 8, 10; mandatory school-level analysis of results by family background), but PISA findings have not historically translated into structural policy changes despite government use of PISA data to legitimize reforms
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Missing link between data collection and structural correction is inferred rather than directly demonstrated
Causality between measurement gaps and policy inaction not definitively established
Recent 2026 ministerial initiative may represent a change in this historical pattern, making current status uncertain
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
